items.briefing:
Australia's Privacy Commissioner has found that Optus breached privacy laws by incorrectly listing customers in the White Pages without their consent — a reminder that organisations must handle personal data carefully and comply with privacy obligations. Separately, Microsoft has signed a digital resilience deal with the Australian government, signalling increased focus on national cyber preparedness. Chinese and North Korean threat groups are also reported to be expanding their activity across the Asia-Pacific region, meaning Australian organisations should remain alert.

Phishing attacks via Microsoft Teams chat are an increasing concern, with attackers using chat platforms — not just email — to target staff. **Practical tip:** Remind your team to be cautious about unexpected links or requests received via Teams or other chat tools, just as they would with suspicious emails.

featured.title: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy in White Pages listing matter
featured.quote: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy of tens of thousands of Australians.
